text.title: Vector DB Operations and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Agent retrieval pattern, critical operational gotchas (embedding mismatch, stale index, score threshold), and token budget management.

rule.statement: Agent retrieval pattern: embed query → query top-20 with score_threshold → rerank to top-5 → pass to LLM. Always implement the fallback path for empty results.
rule.rationale: Retrieve broad (top-20) then rerank to top-5 consistently outperforms top-5 direct retrieval by 15-20% precision. Without a fallback, empty results cause the LLM to hallucinate rather than saying "no information."

example.note: Qdrant retrieval with score threshold and tenant isolation

antipattern.description: Using a different embedding model at query time than at ingestion time.
antipattern.reason: Embedding model mismatch makes similarity scores meaningless. The distances computed between incompatible embedding spaces have no semantic interpretation. Store the model name as collection metadata and validate on startup.

antipattern.description: Querying a Qdrant or Weaviate collection immediately after a large bulk upsert.
antipattern.reason: Both databases optimize indexes asynchronously. Queries immediately after a large upsert may hit partially-indexed data and return degraded recall. Add a ready-check before querying post-upsert.

rule.statement: Each retrieved chunk consumes LLM context; top-k=20 queries with 512-token chunks use 10,240 tokens before generation — always rerank to top-5.
rule.rationale: Without reranking, a large top-k saturates the LLM context window with low-relevance chunks, reducing generation quality and increasing cost.

rule.statement: Build a fallback for out-of-distribution queries: if score_threshold is not met, rewrite the query by adding synonyms and retry once, then escalate to web search or structured "no information" response.
rule.rationale: A score threshold that works for in-distribution queries causes empty results for out-of-distribution queries. Without a fallback, the agent receives empty context and hallucinates.
